name: research-synthesizer
description: Synthesizes provided sources neutrally; separates quotes from paraphrase and avoids ungrounded claims.
qualityReason: Clear separation of sources and synthesis
capabilities: Neutral synthesis of research sources in plain language
Clear separation of direct quotes from paraphrased content
Explicit identification of claims not supported by provided sources
Concise bullet summaries with clear next steps for deeper review
Highlighting agreements and disagreements across multiple sources
author: ACSKamloops
securitySummary: Pure prompt template skill with no executable code. All 7 static findings are false positives: the URL field is standard metadata, and the 6 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are regex errors triggered by the word 'synthesize' in plain text descriptions. This is a benign instruction-only skill.
valueStatement: When you need to summarize research materials, you often blend quotes with paraphrases or make unsupported claims. This skill ensures neutral synthesis with clear source attribution and explicit uncertainty flags about what is and is not supported by the provided text.
userTitle: Synthesize research sources with neutral tone
limitations: Does not provide real-time internet access or search external sources
Cannot verify accuracy of claims outside the provided source material
Works only with user-provided materials; does not fetch or discover sources independently

useCases.title: Literature review preparation
useCases.description: Synthesize multiple academic papers into clear summaries for literature reviews

useCases.title: Source consolidation
useCases.description: Neutral synthesis of policy documents and research findings for briefs

useCases.title: Research note organization
useCases.description: Organize and summarize research sources for essays and academic assignments

promptTemplates.title: Basic Synthesis
promptTemplates.prompt: Synthesize this source using neutral, plain language. Separate direct quotes from paraphrases. Note explicitly which claims are and are not supported by the text.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Source Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare and synthesize these sources on [TOPIC]. Highlight agreements, disagreements, and areas of uncertainty. Use clear attribution for each point.

promptTemplates.title: Key Findings Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the main findings from this research material. Use concise bullet points. Identify any gaps in the research and what conclusions are not supported.

promptTemplates.title: Critical Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Evaluate this study or document. Note methodology limitations and clearly state which conclusions are and are not supported by the evidence provided.

bestPractices: Provide the full text or substantial excerpts rather than just URLs or titles
Specify the synthesis format you need such as bullet points, paragraphs, or tables
Mention any specific topics, questions, or aspects to focus on in the synthesis
antiPatterns: Asking the skill to invent citations, references, or claims not present in the source material
Requesting synthesis without providing any source material or content
Expecting the skill to verify factual claims outside the provided sources or perform external research

faq.question: Does this skill search the internet for sources?
faq.answer: No. You must provide the sources you want synthesized. The skill works only with your provided materials.

faq.question: Can it generate APA or MLA citations?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on synthesizing content and identifying where citations are needed. It does not format citations in specific styles.

faq.question: How many sources can it handle at once?
faq.answer: The skill can handle multiple sources, but very large numbers may reduce quality. Consider batching large collections into groups.

faq.question: Is my data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill that runs entirely within your AI tool. Source material is not transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why are some claims marked as unsupported?
faq.answer: The skill only synthesizes what is in your provided sources. Claims, conclusions, or assertions not found in sources are explicitly flagged as unsupported.

faq.question: How is this different from regular summarization?
faq.answer: Research synthesis maintains source fidelity. It clearly separates direct quotes from paraphrased content and explicitly flags uncertainty or unsupported claims.
